mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0, or inflation-adjusted earnings growth outperforms bond yields.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of concurrent INDPRO/GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the spread between INDPRO/GDPC1 exceeds 15%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 2,000k units signals weak demand for construction, reducing corporate revenues and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 2000, or housing-related sector returns outperform the index.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k jobs signals a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or wage growth fails to outpace inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals labor market tightness, increasing wage inflation and prompting monetary policy tightening, reducing equity valuations over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.0, or Fed rate hikes fail to materialize.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of concurrent RSAFS/HOUST growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the spread between RSAFS/HOUST exceeds 20%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4% signals monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs and reducing corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0, or bond yields fail to rise in tandem.
